title: Creating Experiments
text: Creating experiment() object requires three key components: Expression Matrix: Numeric data variables rows samples columns Sample Information: tibble containing sample metadata (groups, batches, etc.) Variable Information: tibble describing variables (proteins, peptides, glycan compositions, etc.) also need specify experiment metadata.

title: Creating Experiments
text: sample information table records metadata sample. golden rule simple: first column must named sample, every entry needs unique (duplicates allowed!). sample identifiers – names tie everything together. remaining columns, complete freedom! Add information matters analysis: groups, batches, patient demographics, treatment conditions – whatever helps tell story.

title: Creating Experiments
text: glycoverse ecosystem uses standard column names: group: Experimental conditions treatments (use factor type). glystats functions rely column. batch: Sample batch information (use factor type). Essential glyclean::remove_batch_effect(). batch column exists, glyclean::auto_clean() skips batch correction. bio_rep: Biological replicates (use factor type). Note: function automatically coerces column types expected types don’t match. behavior can controlled coerce_col_types check_col_types arguments. Manual conversion recommended finer control details like factor levels. Let’s build sample information table:
code: sample_info <- tibble(   sample = c("S1", "S2", "S3", "S4", "S5", "S6"),   group = factor(c("A", "A", "A", "B", "B", "B"), levels = c("A", "B")),   batch = factor(c(1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2), levels = c(1, 2)) ) sample_info #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   sample group batch #>   <chr>  <fct> <fct> #> 1 S1     A     1     #> 2 S2     A     2     #> 3 S3     A     1     #> 4 S4     B     2     #> 5 S5     B     1     #> 6 S6     B     2

title: Creating Experiments
text: variable information table describes measurement represents. glycoproteomics: ’re using glyread, variable information automatically extracted software output files. glycomics: Variable information may need built manually. Glycomics data structures simpler glycoproteomics.

title: Creating Experiments
text: first column must named variable unique values. Names don’t need meaningful - glyread uses simple names like “V1”, “V2”, “V3” default.

title: Creating Experiments
text: glycomics experiments: glycan_composition: Required. Glycan composition glyrepr::glycan_composition() object glycan_structure: Optional. Glycan structure glyrepr::glycan_structure() object (Parsed objects recommended character strings avoid repeated parsing) glycoproteomics experiments: protein: Required. UniProt accession (character) protein_site: Required. Glycosylation site position protein (integer) gene: Optional. Gene name (character) peptide: Optional. Peptide sequence (character) peptide_site: Optional. Peptide site position glycan attachment (integer) Let’s create variable information table:
code: var_info <- tibble(   variable = c("V1", "V2", "V3"),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(     c(GalNAc = 1),     c(Gal = 1, GalNAc = 1),     c(Gal = 1, GalNAc = 1, GlcNAc = 1)   ),   glycan_structure = glyrepr::as_glycan_structure(c(     "GalNAc(a1-",     "Gal(b1-3)GalNAc(a1-",     "Gal(b1-3)[GlcNAc(a1-6)]GalNAc(a1-"   )) ) var_info #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   variable glycan_composition       glycan_structure                  #>   <chr>    <comp>                   <struct>                          #> 1 V1       GalNAc(1)                GalNAc(a1-                        #> 2 V2       Gal(1)GalNAc(1)          Gal(b1-3)GalNAc(a1-               #> 3 V3       Gal(1)GlcNAc(1)GalNAc(1) Gal(b1-3)[GlcNAc(a1-6)]GalNAc(a1-

title: Creating Experiments
text: expression matrix contains actual measurements. Layout: variables rows, samples columns. transformation (like log-transformation) needed - glycoverse functions handle internally required.

title: Creating Experiments
text: Row names must match variable column variable information, column names must match sample column sample information. order doesn’t need perfect - functions handle alignment automatically. Let’s create expression matrix:
code: # Create a simple matrix with 3 variables and 6 samples expr_mat <- matrix(   rnorm(18, mean = 10, sd = 2), # Some realistic-looking data   nrow = 3,    ncol = 6 ) rownames(expr_mat) <- var_info$variable colnames(expr_mat) <- sample_info$sample expr_mat #>           S1        S2       S3        S4        S5       S6 #> V1  7.199913  9.988857 6.356365  9.434589 14.130050 6.273977 #> V2 10.510634 11.243105 9.505349  8.892601  6.738021 8.955975 #> V3  5.125473 12.296823 9.511601 11.257964 11.024854 9.894796

title: Creating Experiments
text: Now assemble experiment object: Specify experiment type ("glycomics" "glycoproteomics") glycan type (like "N" N-glycans, "O-GalNAc" O-GalNAc glycans, etc.). details help downstream functions interpret data correctly.
code: exp <- experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info, exp_type = "glycomics", glycan_type = "N") exp #>  #> ── Glycomics Experiment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 6 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: group <fct>, batch <fct> #> ℹ Variable information fields: glycan_composition <comp>, glycan_structure <struct>

title: Creating Experiments
text: minimum required input expression matrix. information provided, automatically generated based following rules: sample_info: tibble one column named “sample”, column names expr_mat. var_info: tibble one column named “variable”, row names expr_mat. exp_type: “others” glycan_type: NULL means can create experiment() objects flexibly: first create backbone using expression matrix, add information later using mutate_var() mutate_obs(). data multiple tables, use left_join_var() left_join_obs() join .
code: expr_mat <- matrix(runif(9), nrow = 3, ncol = 3) colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2", "S3") rownames(expr_mat) <- c("V1", "V2", "V3") experiment(expr_mat) #>  #> ── Others Experiment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 3 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: none

what: Core Philosophy: One Action, Three Updates
title: dplyr-Style Functions: Data Harmony in Action
text: glyexp’s dplyr-style functions work three components: Expression Matrix: Numerical data Sample Info: Experimental metadata Variable Info: Molecular annotations traditional data analysis, filtering samples requires manually updating related tables. glyexp’s dplyr-style functions handle synchronization automatically. ’s example:
code: toy_exp <- toy_experiment print(toy_exp) #>  #> ── Others Experiment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 6 samples, 4 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: group <chr>, batch <dbl> #> ℹ Variable information fields: protein <chr>, peptide <chr>, glycan_composition <chr>

title: dplyr-Style Functions: Data Harmony in Action
text: Every dplyr-style function glyexp comes two variants: _obs() functions: Work sample information experiment() objects _var() functions: Work variable annotations experiment() objects variants automatically update expression matrix maintain synchronization. functions require experiment() object input return experiment() object output. standard tibbles data.frames, use regular dplyr functions directly.

what: Why Doesn’t glyexp Implement All dplyr Functions?
title: dplyr-Style Functions: Data Harmony in Action
text: glyexp implements functions preserve synchronized multi-table structure experiment() objects. Functions like count(), distinct(), summarise(), pull() return aggregated results break original data relationships. operations, extract relevant tibble use standard dplyr functions:
code: # For complex aggregations toy_exp |>   get_sample_info() |>   count(group) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   group     n #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 A         3 #> 2 B         3 # For distinct values toy_exp |>   get_var_info() |>   distinct(protein) |>   pull(protein) #> [1] "PRO1" "PRO2" "PRO3" # For advanced filtering with multiple conditions complex_filter_conditions <- toy_exp |>   get_sample_info() |>   filter(group == "A", batch == 2) |>   pull(sample)  # Then use the results to subset your experiment filtered_by_complex <- filter_obs(toy_exp, sample %in% complex_filter_conditions)

text: glyexp’s dplyr-style functions designed fast, safe, consistent. large datasets, consider: Filtering early pipeline reduce data size Using select_obs() select_var() keep needed columns Chaining operations efficiently minimize intermediate copies

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: Expression matrix: Must numeric matrix variables rows samples columns. column names must correspond sample IDs. row names must correspond variable IDs. Sample information (sample_info): Must tibble column named "sample" (sample ID). value "sample" must unique. set "sample" values must match column names expression matrix (order matter). Variable information (var_info): Must tibble column named "variable" (variable ID). value "variable" must unique. set "variable" values must match row names expression matrix (order matter). function automatically reorder expression matrix match order "sample" "variable" info tables.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: columns required compulsorily variable information tibble valid experiment. depends experiment type. "glycomics": glycan_composition. "glycoproteomics": protein, protein_site, glycan_composition. "traitomics": required columns. "traitproteomics": protein, protein_site. "others": required columns. See "Column conventions" section detailed description columns. last two types experiments created glydet package. Normally need manually create .

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: glycoverse conserved column names sample_info var_info make everything work seamlessly. mandatory, following conventions make life easier. sample_info: group: factor, treatment/condition/grouping, used many glystats glyvis functions. batch: factor, batch information, used glyclean::correct_batch_effect(). bio_rep: factor, biological replicate, may used future. var_info: protein: character, protein Uniprot accession. protein_site: integer, glycosylation site position protein. gene: character, gene symbol. peptide: character, peptide sequence. peptide_site: integer, glycosylation site position peptide. glycan_composition: glyrepr::glycan_composition(), glycan composition. glycan_structure: glyrepr::glycan_structure(), glycan structure.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: meta data can added meta_data attribute. meta_data list additional information experiment. Two meta data fields required: exp_type: "glycomics", "glycoproteomics", "traitomics", "traitproteomics", "others" glycan_type: "N", "O-GalNAc", "O-GlcNAc", "O-Man", "O-Fuc", "O-Glc", NULL meta data added glycoverse packages purposes.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
text: index columns backbone keep data synchronized: "sample" column sample_info must match column names expr_mat. "variable" column var_info must match row names expr_mat. columns act unique identifiers, ensuring expression matrix, sample information, variable information always stay sync, matter filter, arrange, subset data.

title: Create a new experiment — experiment
code: # The minimum required input is an expression matrix. expr_mat <- matrix(runif(9), nrow = 3, ncol = 3) colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2", "S3") rownames(expr_mat) <- c("V1", "V2", "V3") experiment(expr_mat) #>  #> ── Others Experiment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 3 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: none  # Or with more detailed information. sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2", "S3"), group = c("A", "B", "A")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("V1", "V2", "V3"),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 1)) ) experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info, exp_type = "glycomics", glycan_type = "N") #> Column group converted to <factor>. #>  #> ── Glycomics Experiment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 3 samples, 3 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: group <fct> #> ℹ Variable information fields: glycan_composition <comp>

what: glyexp: Glycoproteomics and Glycomics Experiments — glyexp-package
title: glyexp: Glycoproteomics and Glycomics Experiments — glyexp-package
text: Provides tidy data framework managing glycoproteomics glycomics experimental data. core feature 'experiment()' class, serves unified data container integrating expression matrices, variable information (proteins, peptides, glycan compositions, etc.), sample metadata (groups, batches, clinical variables, etc.). package enforces data consistency, validates column types according experiment types (glycomics, glycoproteomics, traitomics, traitproteomics), provides dplyr-style data manipulation functions (filter, mutate, select, arrange, slice, join) seamless data wrangling. data core 'glycoverse' ecosystem, provides consistent interface packages can reliably extract information , enabling smooth data exchange analysis workflows.

what: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
text: Converts meaningless variable IDs (like "GP1", "V1") meaningful, human-readable IDs based experiment type available columns. format new IDs depends exp_type format specified: glycomics: {glycan_composition}, e.g., "Hex(5)HexNAc(2)" glycoproteomics: {protein}-{protein_site}-{glycan_composition} traitomics: {motif} {trait} depending column present traitproteomics: {protein}-{protein_site}-{motif} {protein}-{protein_site}-{trait} duplicate IDs generated (e.g., composition multiple PSMs), unique integer suffix appended using unique_suffix pattern.

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
code: standardize_variable(exp, format = NULL, unique_suffix = "-{N}")

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
text: exp experiment(). format format string specifying construct variable IDs. Use {column_name} insert values var_info columns. example, "{gene}-{glycan_composition}" produce "GENE1-Hex(5)". NULL (default), sensible format chosen based exp_type. unique_suffix string pattern making IDs unique duplicates exist. Must contain {N} replaced numeric suffix (1, 2, 3...). Default "-{N}" produces IDs like "Hex(5)-1", "Hex(5)-2".

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable

title: Standardize variable IDs in an experiment — standardize_variable
code: # Glycomics example expr_mat <- matrix(1:4, nrow = 2) rownames(expr_mat) <- c("V1", "V2") colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2") sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("V1", "V2"),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 5, HexNAc = 2)) ) exp <- experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info,   exp_type = "glycomics", glycan_type = "N" ) standardize_variable(exp) #>  #> ── Glycomics Experiment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: glycan_composition <comp>  # Glycoproteomics example expr_mat <- matrix(1:4, nrow = 2) rownames(expr_mat) <- c("GP1", "GP2") colnames(expr_mat) <- c("S1", "S2") sample_info <- tibble::tibble(sample = c("S1", "S2")) var_info <- tibble::tibble(   variable = c("GP1", "GP2"),   protein = c("P12345", "P12345"),   protein_site = c(32L, 45L),   glycan_composition = glyrepr::glycan_composition(c(Hex = 5, HexNAc = 2)) ) exp <- experiment(expr_mat, sample_info, var_info,   exp_type = "glycoproteomics", glycan_type = "N" ) standardize_variable(exp) #>  #> ── Glycoproteomics Experiment ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: protein <chr>, protein_site <int>, glycan_composition <comp>  # Custom format example standardize_variable(exp, format = "{protein}-{glycan_composition}") #>  #> ── Glycoproteomics Experiment ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Expression matrix: 2 samples, 2 variables #> ℹ Sample information fields: none #> ℹ Variable information fields: protein <chr>, protein_site <int>, glycan_composition <comp>
